description: Existing in or derived from nature; not made or caused by humankind. 'Woeli' names the quality of belonging to the living world—authentic, unforced, arising from the patterns of nature rather than human imposition.
sound_symbolism: The word grows from earth. 'Wo' begins with organic roundness, nature's curves. The vowel hiatus 'o.e' opens to authenticity, nothing hidden. Then 'li' lives with liquid aliveness, the natural state.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'woeli pelowa' (natural meadow). Related to 'shaemu' (organic) but broader—includes all that belongs to nature's patterns.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional life aligned with natural patterns—natural materials, natural rhythms, natural relationships.
pillars.solarpunk-values: Natural systems provide models for sustainable design—biomimicry learns from nature's solutions.
